arguments.task: Open official Help Scout developer documentation and first-party pricing pages. Read pages that establish: API authentication methods, API types/surfaces, rate limits, app/platform docs, any official MCP/server references, and pricing/access needed to use the API. Prefer canonical first-party URLs that load in browser. Return a concise evidence-focused summary with the exact page URLs visited and the facts each page supports. Also inspect Help Scout pricing pages on helpscout.com for plan/self-serve evidence relevant to API availability.

input.thought: Start browser research on official Help Scout docs and pricing pages to gather evidence-backed structured fields.

raw_result.description: Customer support platform with APIs for inboxes, docs, apps, beacons, and webhooks.
raw_result.auth_notes: Help Scout documents OAuth 2.0 for the Mailbox/Inbox API, including authorization code and client credentials flows, and documents an API key for the Docs API.
raw_result.gating_reason: Help Scout pricing indicates API access is not included on the Free plan and is available on paid plans such as Standard, Plus, and Pro.

api_surface.breadth: Public REST APIs for Mailbox/Inbox and Docs, plus webhooks, apps, and Beacon-related developer platform surfaces.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official Help Scout MCP server documentation was established from the gathered first-party evidence.

raw_result.blocker: A first toolkit is buildable from public docs, but paid-plan API access is required because the Free plan lacks API access.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Mailbox API rate limits observed from official docs/pricing: developer docs show 200 requests per minute, and pricing indicates higher API limits by paid tier such as 200/min on Standard, 400/min on Plus, and 800/min on Pro. Docs API notes observed by browser indicated 2000-4000 requests per 10 minutes depending on endpoint/account context.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official developer site clearly organizes Apps, Beacon, Inbox API, Docs API, and Webhooks, which is a strong signal for agent/toolkit coverage.
raw_result.human_review_reason: mcp_exists is a structured field not directly supported by a specific first-party page in the gathered evidence; absence of an official MCP reference was inferred from browsing rather than verified by a canonical MCP/help page.
